sub_mechanisms: M_1: cross-layer coupling matrix between residual stream layers
M_3: feedback-attenuation gate driven by the symmetric L
M_4: Lyapunov-style stability certificate for the residual update
stripped_llm_application: Residual-stream updates are coupled across layers through a symmetric matrix L whose entries satisfy a reciprocity constraint (L_ij=L_ji). The symmetry forces positive-semidefinite drift and yields a closed-form Lyapunov function for the relaxation dynamics; an attenuation gate dampens cross-layer feedback when the Lyapunov derivative is positive.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing cross-layer methods (residual connections, layer-norm scaling, gradient routing) do not enforce a symmetric reciprocity constraint on the cross-layer coupling. The Lyapunov certificate from symmetry is new.
